title: brassica campestris subvar. depressa
intro: Brassica campestris subvar. depressa, also known as rutabaga, is a cool-season crop that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is a cross between a cabbage and a turnip. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain cancers and improving cardiovascular health. Rutabaga can be consumed raw or cooked and is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Additionally, rutabaga has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it a useful addition to herbal remedies for wound healing and skin care.
